headline: BIS warns AI frenzy could trigger market slump
detail: The Bank for International Settlements cautioned that the AI investment boom risks creating instability that could spill into broader markets. This is a direct shot across the bow for your entire portfolio — AVGO, PLTR, CRWD, SMH, QQQ are all priced with AI growth baked in. If institutional money takes this seriously, high-multiple tech names reprice fast.

headline: Severe weather emerging as threat to AI boom
detail: Infrastructure risks from extreme weather are being flagged as a genuine constraint on AI buildout timelines. This matters for chip demand expectations — AVGO and SMH are both leveraged to data center and AI chip spending cycles. Any slowdown in that capex story hurts your core names.

headline: South Korea: Samsung and SK Hynix investing in AI and chip mega-projects
detail: This is a longer-term positive signal for semiconductor demand, which supports SMH — your one HOLD with actual gains (up 10%). It confirms the structural story isn't dead, just under pressure from valuation and macro concerns right now.

headline: China widens export curbs, targeting Japan in defense and tech sectors
detail: Escalating tech trade restrictions add geopolitical risk to the semiconductor supply chain. SMH and AVGO both have exposure to cross-border chip flows. This is a slow-burn risk, but in a nervous market it can be used as a reason to sell first and ask questions later.
